title: Quockran
content:

{{Quote|Why certainly, sir, I'd be glad to help, but I must tell you that I really don't know very much about this place. You see, we're all droids here.|A Quockran droid hides the Quockrans' existence|<ref name="GG6" />}}
The '''Quockrans''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Slug (creature)/Legends|slugs]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Quockra-4]]. The species had a large, fat body that tapered to a tail. Their torso also had two arms, ending in stubby digits. The [[black]]-skinned Quockrans had no differentiated internal organs. During their evolution, their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was a moist planet. Nevertheless, the climate changed, becoming dry and hot. This drove the Quockrans' society underground, apart from short forays to the surface. The Quockrans were a [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] species, desiring isolation from all other species. When the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]] was colonized late in the administration of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], offworlders reached Quockra-4. The Quockrans responded to this violation of their isolation by manufacturing an entire society of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] to act as intermediaries with the offworlders. These droids traded with offworlders, largely keeping the existence of the Quockrans a secret.

==Biology and appearance==
The Quockrans were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of giant [[Slug (creature)/Legends|slugs]].<ref name="GG6" /> Their bodies had a large, fat torso that tapered to a tail marked with large rolls of fat. Quockrans averaged between 1.4 and 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length,<ref name="AE" /> although some had the ability grow as long as two meters.<ref name="GG6" /> The Quockrans had a significant paunch and large drooping breasts. They had two arms that ended in four stubby fingers. The slug-like creatures had [[black]] skin, with splotches and other growths running down their midline and tail, as well as down the top of their arms. They had no differentiated internal organs,<ref name="AE" /> which limited the injury a single wound could do to their bodies.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|65}}</ref>

Quockran heads appeared knobbly. Each Quockran had a nose high on its forehead, with its [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] below this, one on each side of its heads. Below its nose was a large mouth, filled with sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. Quockran chins were large and drooping.<ref name="AE" />

==Society and culture==
The Quockrans were a [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] species, avoiding contact with other species if at all possible. While they despised offworlders, this rarely manifested as violence toward them. They were indifferent to [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] affairs and refused to get involved in [[Politics/Legends|political]] causes such as the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]]. If a Quockran was forced to interact with other species, they were surly and uncooperative, preferring, simply, to be left alone.<ref name="GG6" />

To facilitate their isolation, the Quockrans manufactured an entire society of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] to interact with offworlders<ref name="AE" /> in their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld's]] sole [[Quockra-4 city|city]]. Composed of thousands of different designs, both [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] models and other, more exotic styles, and numbering over ten million individual units,<ref name="GG6" /> the droids appeared to manage all the affairs of [[Quockra-4]].<ref name="AE" /> The automata followed a clearly delineated hierarchy: three-armed [[yellow repair droid]]s were superior, giving out orders in their [[Binary/Legends|machine language]]; [[Red worker droid|red worker models]] were in the middle; Imperial droids were at the bottom, functioning as translators for those of higher rank.<ref name="GG6" /> With this system in place, the Quockrans were able to live far below ground, only emerging on the surface at night to loll about in the cooling desert sands.<ref name="GG6" />

==History==
The Quockrans evolved on Quockra-4,<ref name="GG6" /> the fourth [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Quockra system/Legends|Quockra system]], located in the remote [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]] of the southern [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> The planet was originally moist, and the Quockrans lived on the surface. The climate changed,<ref name="AE" /> however, becoming a homogeneous desert, with several large [[Salt/Legends|salt]] flats. The surface temperature reached polar extremes, with soaring highs during the day and frigid temperatures at night.<ref name="GG6" /> This drove the Quockrans deep underground, although they still occasionally emerged at night to enjoy the cooler, damper air.<ref name="AE" />

When the Minos Cluster was colonized, late in the administration of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the Quockrans created a droid society to interact with the newly arrived offworlders. The Quockrans used these droids as intermediaries for trading,<ref name="GG6" /> and most inhabitants of the Minos Cluster were unaware the species existed.<ref name="CSWE" /> Any who were aware of the Quockrans' existence and who tried to interfere with their affairs quickly found themselves in trouble.<ref name="GG6" />

{{RPG}}
Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], a [[Unidentified Quockran researcher|researcher]] from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] tried to discover the "real natives of Quockra-4." To facilitate his research, he offered a reward of up to [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|3,000]] credits to traders for information on the architects of the exotic droids on Quockra-4. This led to some adventurers exploring tunnels below the planet's surface, encountering numerous groups of droids along the way—who progressively ignored them, ordered them to leave, and finally attacked them—before discovering Quockrans. These Quockrans refused to communicate with the adventurers.<ref name="GG6" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Ytha.jpg|thumb|right|upright|The "Ytha" in ''Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas'']]
{{Quote|You: fast moving, multi-appendaged, literate. Age not important, chins a must. I like scrapping asteroids, quiet plows on the beach, laser-lit feeding cycles, film. Hobbies include basket puncturing, lip moisturizing, magnetic burglaries. I want more out of existence than wind harassment and empty teeth locks. If you wake up at night wanting someone to cuddle you, send me a signal.|The Ytha's personal advertisement in ''Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas''|<ref name="Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas">''[[Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas]]''</ref>}}
The Quockrans were created by [[Mark Rein-Hagen]] and [[Stewart Wieck]] for ''[[Galaxy Guide 6: Tramp Freighters]]'', a [[roleplaying game]] sourcebook published by [[West End Games]] in [[1990]]. This book, and its [[Galaxy Guide 6: Tramp Freighters (Second Edition)|second edition]], include an adventure seed in which players try to discover the Quockran species, although no [[canon]]ical ending for this encounter is specified. This information was republished, albeit slightly condensed, in ''[[Alien Encounters]]'' in [[1998]]. ''Alien Encounters'' changes their height from the two meters established in ''Galaxy Guide 6: Tramp Freighters'' to 1.4–1.7 meters.

''Alien Encounters'' also includes an illustration of a Quockran. The image used is a reworking of early concept art by [[Ralph McQuarrie]] of the character [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]], who appears in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], first released in [[1983]], and who has been identified as a member of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] species. The concept art was published in ''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi]]'' later that same year.<ref name="The Art of Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi]]''</ref> A mirrored version of the same image by McQuarrie is included in ''[[Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas]]'', a non-canonical work published in [[1993]], which collects concept designs created in various [[George Lucas]] projects. This book accompanies the image with a small blurb in the form of a personal advertisement. This identifies the species as a "Ytha" and requests a "hard-shelled creature for friendship / possible relationship."<ref name="Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas" />

In the West End Games roleplaying game, Quockrans are given statistics for players to use them as their own characters. Compared with the average [[Human/Legends|Human]], their perception attribute is slightly higher, while their technical skills are significantly higher. Their knowledge and dexterity scores are slightly lower than Humans, while their mechanical- and strength-based attributes are significantly lower. An exception is made to this low strength score when resisting damage, for which their score is significantly higher than the average Human.<ref name="AE + TRG">''[[Alien Encounters]]'' compared with the Human attributes in ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref>
